title: angophora intermedia var. melanoxylon
intro: Angophora intermedia var. melanoxylon, also known as the Rough-barked Apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to eastern Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Angophora intermedia var. melanoxylon typically involve boiling the bark and leaves in water to create a tea that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
